payload.summary: ULIDs chosen over UUIDs for timestamp-sortability. Agents create facts in time order — ULID encoding preserves this, making chronological queries free. 26-char Crockford base32 is also more compact than UUID hex

Spans and edges share one JSONL stream via a tagged union (serde tag=t). Alternative was separate files for spans vs edges. Single stream preserves creation order, simplifies append logic, and means one file per namespace

Append-only design: edges are never modified or deleted. Corrections happen via supersedes edges. This gives full provenance (what was believed and when), crash-safe writes (no partial updates), and trivial git merging (append = no conflicts)

SQLite index is disposable — deleted and rebuilt from JSONL on every update. JSONL is the source of truth. This means: index.db should be gitignored, schema changes are non-breaking (just rebuild), and JSONL files are the backup/migration format

snippet: /// Implements the 10-signal ranking policy from spec §10.3.

payload.summary: Ranking uses 10 weighted signals summing to 1.0 rather than a single sort key. Rationale: different contexts value different qualities (security audits want high confidence, code review wants recency). Weights are hardcoded now but designed to become configurable per-namespace

Payloads are freeform JSON with conventional keys (summary, confidence, action, tags) accessed via PayloadEnvelope. Alternative was a typed struct. Freeform chosen because: agents discover new fields over time, domain-specific keys should not require schema changes, graceful degradation (unknown keys ignored, missing keys return None)

snippet: /// Path to the JSONL file for a given namespace.

payload.summary: One JSONL file per namespace. Isolation is at the filesystem level, not query-level filtering. This means: namespaces can be deleted by removing a file, merged by concatenation, and shared by copying. Git diff shows exactly which namespace changed

snippet: /// Meta-relationship types that operate on edges rather than spans.

payload.summary: Endorsements, disputes, supersessions are edges pointing at other edges — not boolean fields on content edges. This preserves append-only semantics (endorsing does not mutate the target), allows multiple endorsements with different agents/timestamps, and makes provenance queryable via the same graph traversal

Exponential decay 0.5^(age/half_life) chosen over linear or step-function. Rationale: smooth degradation, no cliff edges, half_life_days=0 cleanly disables it. Endorsements reset effective_ts — re-confirming a finding makes it fresh again without mutation

Rels are classified into 4 tiers: Judgment(1.0) > Other(0.8) > Descriptive(0.6) > Meta(0.0). Rationale: damask at should surface findings and risks before descriptions and dependencies. Meta rels are structural — weight 0.0 means they never pollute content queries. Unknown rels get Other(0.8) so custom rels are not penalized

Ranking uses 10 weighted signals summing to 1.0 — but per the Bitter Lesson, this is temporary infrastructure. The graph structure (spans, edges, relationships, endorsements, timestamps) is the durable asset. The ranking function is swappable. As damask graphs accumulate at scale, a learned ranking (cross-encoder trained on endorsement/dispute signals) will outperform hand-tuned weights. Do not over-engineer the ranking — accumulate the graph.

0.8 revision: adds Bitter Lesson insight — ranking is temporary, graph is durable. Original correctly described the weights; this extends with the design philosophy that weights should eventually become learned.

payload.action: Add commit: Option<String> to Span struct with serde skip_serializing_if
payload.summary: 0.8: Add optional commit field to Span for git-anchored resolution. Enables 4-level degradation cascade: commit:path:lines (exact) → commit:path (relocate within file) → commit:new_path (git rename detection) → unresolved (payload preserved). Delegates re-anchoring to git diff/blame/follow rather than reinventing content tracking. Non-git content omits the field and falls back to existing multi-anchor resolution.

briefing command exists for the Claude Code SessionStart hook: token-capped markdown digest injected at session start, eliminating cold-start re-exploration. Fails open (exit 0, silent) on any error because a hook must never break a session.

payload.action: If transcript format changes upstream, update scan_transcript tool_use parsing
payload.summary: harvest command exists for the Claude Code Stop hook: scans the session transcript and blocks the stop once (stop_hook_active guards re-entry) when files were edited but no damask write command ran. Recording is detected by Bash command substring match (damask record/edge/span/batch/endorse/dispute/close). Every error path allows the stop — fail open is a hard requirement for hooks.

payload.reasoning: Found during live spin: the previous session recorded edges via damask --ns decisions record, which the substring detector missed — harvest would have nudged despite findings being recorded.
payload.summary: Damask-write detection in harvest is token-based, not substring: agents invoke damask as ./target/debug/damask or with global flags before the subcommand (damask --ns x record), which defeated the original "damask record" substring match. False positives only suppress the nudge, so loose matching errs the right way.

snippet: /// True if a shell command invokes damask with a write subcommand.

payload.reasoning: Found during live spin: the previous session recorded edges via damask --ns decisions record, which the substring detector missed.
payload.summary: Damask-write detection in harvest is token-based, not substring: agents invoke damask as ./target/debug/damask or with global flags before the subcommand (damask --ns x record), which defeated the original "damask record" substring match. False positives only suppress the nudge, so loose matching errs the right way.
Closed — incorrect, investigation determined this is not accurate

snippet: pub fn run(file: Option<&str>, prompt: Option<&str>, session: Option<&str>) -...

payload.summary: peek is one command serving two hook events, dispatched on hook_event_name from stdin JSON: PostToolUse (file context after Read/Edit) and UserPromptSubmit (FTS keyword match on the prompt). Session-scoped seen-cache at .damask/.session/<id>.seen guarantees each edge is injected at most once per session; the directory is self-gitignoring for projects whose .gitignore predates it.

snippet: //! Verifiable edges: claims that defend themselves.

payload.summary: verify runs payload check shell commands (sh -c from repo root, polled timeout) and with --auto appends endorsement/dispute meta-edges. Growth is bounded: each outcome kind is recorded at most once per edge, detected via edges_targeting (NOT edges_from — meta-edges are is_active=0 in the index).

Incremental index mtimes must be nanosecond precision. Whole-second as_secs() made JSONL appends invisible to the next index update when they landed in the same second as the previous one — exactly the cadence of rapid sequential agent commands. Found when verify --auto duplicated meta-edges.
check passed: `grep -q as_nanos crates/damask-store/src/index/build.rs`

snippet: //! Optional pairing with ck (https://github.com/BeaconBay/ck), a semantic

payload.summary: ck pairing is strictly optional and one-directional in dependency: damask exports edge summaries to .damask/knowledge/<ns>/<edge_id>.md (auto-managed, stamped, self-gitignored) and shells to ck --sem --jsonl --no-ignore; result basenames map back to edge IDs. search --sem falls back to FTS with an install hint when ck is absent; enrich accepts any {path, span|line} JSONL. Nothing in damask core may depend on ck.

Running ck directly against crates/ creates crates/.ck which breaks the Cargo workspace glob members=["crates/*"] (cargo tries to load .ck as a member and fails). Root .gitignore now ignores .ck/; damask keeps its own ck index inside .damask/knowledge/ where it is harmless.

Installed skill copies are content-compared against the binary-embedded canonical text: init rewrites only on drift (reporting created/updated/already current) and briefing warns at session start when the installed SKILL.md is stale. Motivated by a real failure: an agent worked from a stale skill copy and burned an attempt on bad batch syntax.

Two-tier knowledge lifecycle: descriptive/structural knowledge is SCAFFOLDING — regenerate per-epoch via bootstrap/fan-out into ephemeral namespaces, truncate freely (anchor rot data: ungardened stores lost 15-19% of anchors to deletion within months, and repo clones fork their stores). Retention earns its cost only for the judgment core — decisions, negative results, gotchas, verified risks — where anchor rot is not truth rot and regeneration reproduces conclusions wrongly, not just expensively. Formalize: mark namespaces ephemeral vs durable in config; triage and trust accounting should ignore ephemeral namespaces.
